titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Walter Francis Willcox papers, 1886-1960.
titlestmt.author: Compiled by N. Dean

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library

titlepage.titleproper: Guide to the Walter Francis Willcox papers,1886-1960
titlepage.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections Cornell University Library

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu
http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

titlepage.date: © 2002 Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library

did.head: DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

physdesc.label: Quantity:
physdesc.extent: 7 cubic ft.; 16 reels negative microfilm; 4 tape recordings; 4 folders.

did.repository: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
did.abstract: Papers concerning Willcox's career as professor of economics and statistics at Cornell, including extensive material on American social and political issues, as well as Willcox's personal and family papers.

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of economics and statistics at Cornell University.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, tributes, printed and draft articles and notes, and genealogies and other family papers. Subjects include Willcox's involvement in the United States census of 1900, the International Statistical Institute, Amherst College alumni activity, and statistical analysis of various social problems, including the registration of births and deaths and voter registration, Afro-American crime statistics, race relations, and Prohibition.

The collection also includes tape recordings celebrating Willcox's 90th, 95th, and 100th birthdays; family correspondence, including letters to and from Alice Eloise Work (Mrs. Walter Willcox), and his son Bertram F. who was in France with the American Ambulance Field Service.

Also, material relating to his work with the William Allen White Committee to Defend America by Aiding the Allies, comment on American political practices and statecraft, addresses and articles pertaining to Cornell University, and work concerning Congressional reapportionment. Tape recordings pertain to Andrew Dickson White, Jacob Gould Schurman, and Charles Kendall Adams.

controlaccess.head: Names:
controlaccess.corpname: William Allen White Committee to Defend America by Aiding the Allies.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Includes collections #14-10-821, #14-10-mf32, #14-10-tr24, tr25, tr23, and tr22, and #14-10-m.76, m.376, and m.466.

did.unittitle: St-W correspondence re statistics and reapportionment for U.S. Congress
Genealogical material re folder of Wilcox family letters, 1804-1848? ; Statistical notes
W correspondence (personal and family). Including Anne Goodenow letters (typescript, 2 folders) and 2 envelopes of letters to Mary A. Wilcox re genealogy
Holmes Family. Genealogy and Biography Notes
Holmes Family. Career of John Holmes
Holmes Family. Description of Holmes House, Alfred, Maine
Willcox Family. Recollections of Willcox Home, Kennebunk, Maine, by Mary A. Willcox
Willcox Family. Miscellaneous papers (includes "Willcox-Work Family Lines")
"Letters from and about Wm. H. Willcox, Wm. G. Willcox;"
Willcox Family Life by Mary A. Willcox
Alice Eloise Work. Letters and Papers,
Walter F. Willcox. Correspondence, Sir Alfred Zimmern, 1953-1957 ; (also Zimmern's writings)
"Sociedad Mexicana de Geografia y Estadistica," letter, diploma, identification card,
Walter F. Willcox. Tributes on 80th Birthday,
Walter F. Willcox. Correspondence, 90th Birthday, Addresses,
Walter F. Willcox. Correspondence, re Address, "Comments on speech at ninetieth anniversary dinner,
Walter Family Letters re Walter F. Willcox bibliography, Will, and 96th Birthday;
Walter F. Willcox re "95th Birthday Comments,"
Walter F. Willcox, 95th Birthday: Addresses by Robert E. Cushman and Frederick G. Marcham
Walter F. Willcox, Transcript of tape recorded interview, Mar. 23, 1957 (with "William Allan and Mary Willcox interview")
Walter F. Willcox's Autobiographical Material, "Early Life," etc.
Walter F. Willcox's Autobiographical Material, "Confessions of an Octogenarian," "The Stone Will Case," etc.
Walter F. Willcox. Reapportionment Letters to New York Times,
Walter F. Willcox. "A Notable College Class" (Amherst, 1884 )
Walter F. Willcox. "The Teaching of Economics, Political and Social Science at Cornell University"
Clippings: Robert Frost, Franklin D. Roosevelt
Miscellaneous Photos including Alfred House, (Maine), Willcox home, (Ithaca), and Mary (?); Negatives
Scrapbook: Congratulations to Walter F. Willcox from the descendants of his brother William G. Willcox,"
"Sprouts from a Winter Garden," 1947-1954 by Walter F. Willcox (2 copies)

"Methods of Appointing Seats in the House of Representatives" by Walter F. Willcox. Reprint, Journal of the American Statistical Association, Dec. 1954, Vol. 49, pp. 685-695 and other related material ( 1940, 1941 )

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Walter Francis Willcox papers, #14-10-504. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
